title: Ag and Food Statistics: Charting the Essentials - Food Prices and Spending
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: In 2024, U.S. food spending reached $2.58 trillion. In 2025, food-at-home prices rose 2.3 percent. The average annual food price growth over the past two years
excerpt: In 2024, U.S. food spending reached $2.58 trillion. In 2025, food-at-home prices rose 2.3 percent. The average annual food price growth over the past two years was 2.6 percent.

content.introduction: In 2024, U.S. food spending reached $2.58 trillion. In 2025, food-at-home prices rose 2.3 percent. The average annual food price growth over the past two years was 2.6 percent.
body: # Food Prices and Spending. In 2024, U.S. consumers, businesses, and government entities spent $2.58 trillion on food and beverages. * ## U.S. food-at-home prices increased 2.3 percent in 2025, compared with 2024. Horizontal comparative bar chart showing annual rates of inflation for major U.S. food-at-home categories from 2024 to 2025. Average annual food-at-home prices were 2.3 percent higher in 2025 than in 2024, less than the 20-year historical rate of 2.6 percent per year. Prices for dairy products increased by 0.8 percent in 2025 compared with a 2.1-percent annual average rate of growth from 2006 through 2025. Prices for cereal and bakery products increased by 1.0 percent in 2025, compared with a 2.8-percent annual average rate of growth over the past 20 years. * ## U.S. food price growth averaged 2.6 percent per year over the past 2 years, 2023 to 2024 and 2024 to 2025.
A collection of over 75 charts and maps presenting key statistics on the farm sector, food spending and prices, food security,
A collection of over 75 charts and maps presenting key statistics on the farm sector, food spending and prices, food security, rural communities,
